zh.lifeSummary: 當Afonso Anes de Riba de Visela於1180年出生在葡萄牙時，他的父親João Fernandes de Riba de Vizela21歲，母親Maria Soares De Sousa31歲。他和Urraca Pires da Ribeira至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1272年3月享年92歲，埋葬在葡萄牙。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Afonso Anes de Riba de Visela于1180年出生在葡萄牙时，他的父亲João Fernandes de Riba de Vizela21岁，母亲Maria Soares De Sousa31岁。他和Urraca Pires da Ribeira至少有2个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1272年3月享年92岁，埋葬在葡萄牙。

nameMeanings.name: Anes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Angel, Dionisio, Esperanza, Gilberto, Ines, Juanita, Luis, Luisa, Manuel, Manuela, Pedro.  Portuguese Joao.
Portuguese and Galician: patronymic from a shortened form of the Latin personal name Johannes (Portuguese João, Galician Xoán; see John ).

variants.name: Ennes
meanings: Portuguese: variant of   Anes  .

variants.name: Enes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Albino, Arlindo, Armando, Gaspar, Jeremias, Marta, Ramon.  Portuguese Ilidio, Joao.
Portuguese (Azores): variant of   Anes  .
In some cases also German: habitational name from a place called Henis in Limburg, eastern Belgium. This surname is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: John
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Riba
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Mendel, Yetta.  German Lothar, Erwin, Nikolaus.  Spanish Jose, Alfredo, Carlos, Claudio, Fidel, Manuel, Ramon.

Catalan: topographic name for someone who lived on the bank of a river or the shore of a lake, riba (from Latin ripa), or a habitational name from La Riba, a town in Alt Camp, Tarragona, or any other place called with this word.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): variant of   Ryba  ‘fish’.

variants.name: Ryba
meanings: Polish, Czech, Slovak, Sorbian, and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name, nickname, or a Jewish artificial name from Polish, Czech, and Slovak ryba ‘fish’. As a metonymic occupational name it may have denoted a fisherman or a seller of fish (see also Ribar , Rybak , and Rybarczyk ). As a nickname it may have been bestowed on account of some fancied physical resemblance to a fish. The Sorbian surname is found mainly in Germanized forms, such as Riebe .

variants.name: Riva
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Digna, Mario, Ramon, Sergio, Alvaro, Angel, Armando, Benito, Cristina, Fernando, Guillermo.  Italian Angelo, Flavio, Agostino, Antonio, Italo, Sal, Santo, Sebastiano.  French Gaston, Jean-Paul.
Italian: topographic name for someone who lived by a wharf or landing at a port or seaport or on the bank of a river, from riva ‘river bank’ (from Latin ripa).
Catalan and Spanish: variant of   Riba  , which has the same origin and meaning as 1 above.

variants.name: Ribas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Eduardo, Jorge, Julio, Alberto, Armando, Carlos, Francisco, Mario, Mirta, Rafael.  Portuguese Joao, Paulo.
Catalan: Castilianized form of Ribes, a habitational name from any of the places called with Catalan ribes, plural of riba ‘bank’ (see Riba ).
Spanish and Galician: habitational name from any of the places called with the plural of riba ‘bank’ (see Riba ). Compare Rivas .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Visela
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Galician and Portuguese: habitational name from any of numerous places called Vilela, from a diminutive of vila ‘village, small town’ (see Vila ). Compare Villela .

variants.name: Vila
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Luis, Juan, Manuel, Jorge, Francisco, Pedro, Ramon, Eduardo, Efrain, Enrique.

Catalan: topographic name for someone who lived in a vila (see 3 below), which in Catalonia denoted a small town or village with certain privileges not common for villages, or a habitational name from any of the places called La Vila or Vila. This is one of the most common surnames in Catalonia.
Catalan (Vilà): variant of Vilar .

variants.name: Villela
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jesus, Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Felipe, Javier, Juan, Raymundo, Alfonso, Eduardo, Geraldo, Jose Cruz.
Spanish and Portuguese: altered form of Galician and Portuguese   Vilela  .

nameMeanings.name: Afonso
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Visigothic: probably a compound of adal ‘noble’ + funs ‘ready’; a traditional royal name.

variants.name: Alonso
meanings: Variant of   Alfonso .

en.lifeSummary: When Afonso Anes de Riba de Visela was born in 1180, in Portugal, his father, João Fernandes de Riba de Vizela, was 21 and his mother, Maria Soares De Sousa, was 31. He had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Urraca Pires da Ribeira. He died in March 1272, at the age of 92, and was buried in Portugal.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Afonso Anes de Riba de Visela est né en 1180, à Portugal, son père, João Fernandes de Riba de Vizela, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Maria Soares De Sousa, avait 31 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Urraca Pires da Ribeira. Il est décédé en mars 1272, à l’âge de 92 ans, et a été enterré à Portugal.

de.lifeSummary: Als Afonso Anes de Riba de Visela 1180 in Portugal geboren wurde, war sein Vater João Fernandes de Riba de Vizela 21 und seine Mutter Maria Soares De Sousa 31. Er hatte mit Urraca Pires da Ribeira mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb im März 1272 im Alter von 92 Jahren und wurde in Portugal begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Afonso Anes de Riba de Visela è nato il nel 1180 nel Portogallo, il suo padre, João Fernandes de Riba de Vizela, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Maria Soares De Sousa, aveva 31 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie con Urraca Pires da Ribeira. Morì nel marzo 1272, all'età di 92 anni, e fu sepolto a Portogallo.

ja.Quinta do Paço de Urgezes em Guimarães,: ポルトガルブラガグイマルaス
ja.Cambra, Aveiro, Portugal: ポルトガルエイベイロカンブラ
ja.lifeSummary: Afonso Anes de Riba de Viselaが1180年にポルトガルで生まれた時，父João Fernandes de Riba de Vizelaは21歳，母Maria Soares De Sousaは31歳であった。彼はUrraca Pires da Ribeiraとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1272年3月に92歳で死去し，ポルトガルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Afonso Anes de Riba de Visela(이)가 1180년 포르투갈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 João Fernandes de Riba de Vizela(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Maria Soares De Sousa(은)는 31세였다. 그는 Urraca Pires da Ribeira(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1272년 3월 92세의 나이로 사망했으며 포르투갈에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Afonso Anes de Riba de Visela nasceu em 1180, em Portugal, seu pai, João Fernandes de Riba de Vizela, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Maria Soares De Sousa, tinha 31 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas com Urraca Pires da Ribeira. Ele faleceu em março de 1272, com 92 anos, e foi sepultado em Portugal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Afonso Anes de Riba de Visela родился в 1180 г. в местности Португалия, его отцу по имени João Fernandes de Riba de Vizela было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Maria Soares De Sousa, 31. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от Urraca Pires da Ribeira. Он умер марта 1272 г. в возрасте 92 лет и был похоронен в местности Португалия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Afonso Anes de Riba de Visela nació en 1180, en Portugal, su papá, João Fernandes de Riba de Vizela, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Maria Soares De Sousa, tenía 31 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas con Urraca Pires da Ribeira. Murió en marzo de 1272, a la edad de 92 años, y fue sepultado en Portugal.
